coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in should test whether your senior engineer is still driving the right priorities or getting pulled into reactive work and hidden dependencies.
openingQuestion: What has changed in your top priorities over the last two weeks, and where are you carrying work that no longer seems like the best use of your time?
listenFor: whether they name a specific project or stakeholder when describing what changed this cycle
if they switch from concrete next steps to general busyness when you ask what is blocked
if they cite a recent update, decision, or missed handoff as an example of how communication is landing
avoid: do not turn the check-in into a status readout you could get from tickets or Slack
do not jump straight to solving blockers before you know which dependencies actually threaten senior-level ownership
goodOutcome: You and Test have agreed which one or two priorities they own most directly as a senior engineer for the next two weeks, plus one dependency or communication gap you will help clear.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pull one recent example where Test’s priorities, dependency handling, or stakeholder updates were visible so you can anchor the conversation in something concrete.
